ORDER TO SHORTEN TIME FOR HEARING OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ISSUANCE OF LETTER OF REQUEST TO TAKE DEOSITION OFN DR. MARTIN DRAXLER; DR. KURT BUSCHING, DR. STEPEHAN U. SYLVESTER; DR. A. BECK; AND DR. WERNER SCHMITZ; IN A FOREIGN NATION; CODE CIV.PROC.2027.010 ET SEQ.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AQUA DEVELOPMENT CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT AQUA'S DUTY TO OPERATE A VALET SERVICE CAUSED THE ACCIDENT. THE UNDISPUTED FACTS ESTABLISH THAT IT WAS NOT THE MANNER IN WHICH THE VALET SERVICE OPERATED THAT CAUSED THE ACCIDENT, IT WAS PECORARO'S DECISION, MADE WITH FULL KNOWLEDGE OF THE FACTS, THAT CAUSED THE ACCIDENT. THE OBJECTION TO AYRES' DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED. ASSUMING ARGUENDO, HE IS EVEN QUALIFIED, THE SUBJECT OF THE DECLARATION IS NOT ONE FOR EXPERT TESTIMONY. MR. STRATMAN IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AQUA DEVELOPMENT CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: FRANK C. LIUZZI, ESQUIRE (LIUZZI MURPHY & SOLOMON, 415.543.5050) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. KEVIN K. CHOLAKIAN, ESQUIRE (CHOLAKIAN & ASSOCIATES, 415.467.8200) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT PECORARO. SCOTT C. STRATMAN, ESQUIRE (VALERIAN, PATTERSON & STRATMAN, 510.521.0612) APPEARING FOR AQUA DEVELOPMENT CORP.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE COURT DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT AQUA'S DUTY TO OPERATE A VALET SERVICE CAUSED THE ACCIDENT. THE UNDISPUTED FACTS ESTABLISH THAT IT WAS NOT THE MANNER IN WHICH THE VALET SERVICE OPERATED THAT CAUSED THE ACCIDENT, IT WAS PECORARO'S DECISION, MADE WITH FULL KNOWLEDGE OF THE FACTS, THAT CAUSED THE ACCIDENT. THE OBJECTION TO AYRES' DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED. ASSUMING ARGUENDO, HE IS EVEN QUALIFIED, THE SUBJECT OF THE DECLARATION IS NOT ONE FOR EXPERT TESTIMONY. MR. STRATMAN IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
